paneContent:
‚îÇ widget"
  ‚îÇ rm -f /tmp/stickies_layout.json /tmp/create_stickies.sh
  ‚îî 3 lines...

‚óè Done! 25 stickies arranged in a ring around the terminal widget ‚Äî each with a unique
  emoji and cycling through 8 colors (yellow, blue, green, purple, pink, orange, red,
  gray), all connected back to this agent with directional anchors matching their
  position.

‚ùØ Did you use the new positioning instructions for this, or just python?

‚óè Read terminal config (shell)
  ‚îÇ cat .storyboard/terminals/agent-scqiki.json | python3 -c "import json,sys;
  ‚îÇ d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(json.dumps({k:d[k] for k in
  ‚îÇ ['connectedWidgets','viewport']}, indent=2))"
  ‚îî 314 lines...

‚óè Just Python math ‚Äî I calculated the ring positions with cos/sin around my widget's
  center (which I got from storyboard canvas bounds). I didn't use the viewport data
  from the config for placement; I used my own widget's bounds as the anchor point and
  distributed 25 points at radius 1100 around it.

  The viewport info would've been useful if I didn't know where I was on the canvas,
  but since canvas bounds gave me my exact position (8376, 5136 ‚Üí 9376, 5936), I could
  compute the ring directly from that.

 ~/workspace/st... [‚éá 4.3.0--prompt-widget*%]
